cause: the design of record claimed the new tab/workspace-ratio scenario closes a gap around the `!window &&` guard on tmux's size flag, and that framing was wrong. The guard is structurally REDUNDANT: size is only spread into the split-window branches and the new-window branch never references it, so deleting the guard changes no emitted argv. Caught by running the mutation the plan's own verification step demanded rather than by reading: removing the guard left all 52 tests green, which is exactly the signature of an inert scenario and would have been reported as one at the impl gate.
fix: re-ran the mutation against the RIGHT wrong subject — an adapter author who wires ...size into the window branch — and confirmed the scenario registers a miss: with size spread into new-window and the guard dropped, both rows of the scenario fail. The scenario is kept because it is loseable and pins the contract the README states (a tab or workspace is never sized); what changed is the account of WHY. The design doc's D7 now records that tmux is defended twice over, that it takes breaking both defenses to lose the row, and that the scenario is tmux-only because herdr's size is lexically scoped inside its pane-split branch and cannot fail it at all. No scenario, test assertion, or production code changed as a result — only the claim about what is under test.
